name: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal USA
mission: TO GENERATE CHARITABLE DONATIONS FOR HELPING IN THE TREATMENT OF CANCER IN THIRD WORLD COUNTRIES AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. SPECIFIC GOALS INCLUDE (1) TO INITIATE CHARITABLE FUND RAISING ACTIVITIES, (2) TO RECRUIT PROFESSIONAL AND NON- PROFESSIONAL STAFF AND EXPENSES FOR THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE, (3) TO SUPPLY FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR THE PAYMENT OF MEDICINES, DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC EQUIPMENT FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. (4) TO ASSIST IN THE RECRUITMENT OF ALL MEDICAL AND ANCILLARY PERSONNEL FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER.
programs: Cancer Registry and Clinical Data Management

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale does not adequately address the significant deduction due to the weak board governance (1 member), which is a key factor in the overall impact score. The rationale mentions capacity building and lack of cost-per-beneficiary data, but the governance issue is a major driver of the lower score and should be explicitly mentioned.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows high alignment with donor interests through its Zakat-eligible status and focus on the urgent global health crisis of cancer in developing regions [2][4]. Its long track record and high Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100 support a strong donor fit [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building by funding clinical and diagnostic services for cancer patients [3]. While it maintains a high program expense ratio of 97.4% and 9.8 months of working capital, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not currently available to reach the highest impact tier [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. provides life-saving oncological care to underserved populations through a highly efficient financial model.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency at $0.01 per $1 raised
Strong financial accountability and transparency ratings
baseline_narrative.summary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. is a non-profit organization dedicated to funding cancer treatment in developing nations, primarily supporting the Shaukat Khanum Memorial Cancer Hospital and Research Centre [3]. The organization is verified as zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations toward healthcare for the needy [2]. With a Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100, the charity demonstrates a high level of transparency and financial health [1].

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization must <cite id="12">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond a single member to ensure proper fiduciary oversight.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fund high-tech medical interventions and cancer treatment for the poor in Pakistan through a financially lean organization.

Value organizations with near-zero administrative overhead

giving_considerations: High revenue growth indicates a rapidly expanding funding gap

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: While outcomes like <cite id="6">6,446 treated neoplasms</cite> are verified, the lack of cost-per-beneficiary data prevents a higher grade.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IKCA funds specialized hospital infrastructure to provide free cancer care to those who cannot afford it, bridging the gap in Pakistan's healthcare system.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that specialized medical equipment and staff are effectively reducing cancer mortality rates.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="4">26.2% revenue growth rate</cite> and expanding hospital services, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new capital.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">97.4% program expense ratio</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="9">peer program ratio median of 88.2%</cite>.
IKCA demonstrates elite fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="10">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The charity holds a <cite id="2">95/100 overall rating</cite> from Charity Navigator, including a <cite id="11">perfect 100 score for Leadership & Adaptability</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal's financial model is optimized for direct service delivery. With <cite id="1">97.4% of expenses dedicated to programs</cite>, the organization bypasses traditional administrative drag. This efficiency is supported by a <cite id="10">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor intent is preserved. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="8">9.8 months of operating reserves</cite>, providing a robust safety net for the specialized medical facilities it supports in Pakistan.
rich_narrative.summary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. (IKCA) serves as a vital financial conduit for providing specialized oncological care to underserved populations in Pakistan, primarily supporting the Shaukat Khanum Memorial Cancer Hospital and Research Centre. The organization operates with an <cite id="1">exceptional program expense ratio of 97.4%</cite>, ensuring that every dollar of public support is directed toward clinical and diagnostic services. IKCA has achieved a <cite id="2">95/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a high standard of accountability and financial health. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $17,974,012</cite>, a figure that has seen significant growth with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 26.2%</cite>. Beyond its financial efficiency, the charity is <cite id="5">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the asnaf categories of the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin). This allows Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations while addressing the critical gap in cancer care in developing nations. The organization's impact is evidenced by its support for complex medical interventions, including <cite id="6">treating 6,446 malignant neoplasms</cite> and <cite id="7">518 pediatric cancer cases</cite> in a single reporting cycle. By maintaining <cite id="8">9.8 months of working capital</cite>, IKCA demonstrates the financial resilience necessary to sustain long-term medical operations in a volatile economic environment.
